title: Where can one get a good End of Eva poster
display_title: Where can one get a good End of Eva poster
wikitext:
[]: Where can one get a good End of Eva poster?             Quote Post by Ari » Sun Jul 05, 2015 8:44 pm Hey! I really, really want a poster version of the cover of the End of Evangelion, but first of all, there are no official ones I can find. Also, the quality of the third-party ones I've found seems to really differ in quality, and I'm not sure I can find one in a good enough quality/size. Would any of you recommend specific places?

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sun Jul 05, 2015 9:20 pm I assume you're talking about the US domestic cover with the giant Rei head -- have never seen that on a larger poster than the ones that came with the DVDs themselves (and those are all creased up). There's a company called Asylum Anime that specializes in authentic Japanese posters and other imported goods and can hunt down vintage stuff for the right price. As regular dealers at Otakon, they've supplied the vast majority of my poster collection. Note that the similarly-named Anime Asylum is a different company. An old otaku that I know also once recommended Anime Jungle as another company that could hunt down import goods. I have a 1997 Japanese theatrical EoE poster that I've debated selling as well, btw.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Mon Jul 06, 2015 7:04 am drinian wrote: I assume you're talking about the US domestic cover with the giant Rei head -- have never seen that on a larger poster than the ones that came with the DVDs themselves (and those are all creased up).

[]:             Quote Post by Ari » Mon Jul 06, 2015 10:48 am I have seen a few like this on Amazon. Thoughts? http://www.amazon.com/gp/aw/d/B00KK6HADY/ref=mp_s_a_1_5?qid=1436197365&sr=8-5&pi=AC_SX110_SY165_QL70&keywords=end+of+evangelion+poster&dpPl=1&dpID=51KKg5CCoHL&ref=plSrch

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Jul 06, 2015 10:53 am That's just an exploded version of the DVD disk cover. I wouldn't spend money on it, but that's me.

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Mon Jul 06, 2015 11:22 am Here's a different question, where could someone get the Japanese poster?

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Mon Jul 06, 2015 3:04 pm hui43210 wrote: Here's a different question, where could someone get the Japanese poster?

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Mon Jul 06, 2015 5:24 pm hui43210 wrote: Here's a different question, where could someone get the Japanese poster?

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Mon Jul 06, 2015 5:36 pm Guy Nacks wrote: I was able to pick up two circa 1997 Death and Rebirth posters which are now framed and in my room. I'm never selling them.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Mon Jul 06, 2015 6:10 pm No, this is one of them: I can't find the other design on an image search, but both have 1997 copyrights on them and are both on the medium-large side.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 06, 2015 10:37 pm Guy Nacks wrote: No, this is one of them: I can't find the other design on an image search, but both have 1997 copyrights on them and are both on the medium-large side.

[]:             Quote Post by Ari » Mon Jul 06, 2015 11:30 pm hm. i might settle for a smaller version of the End of Eva poster, if the only larger ones are blown up. a lot of the eva posters haven't really satisfied me. or, i might get this one: Guy Nacks wrote: No, this is one of them: I can't find the other design on an image search, but both have 1997 copyrights on them and are both on the medium-large side.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Jul 07, 2015 7:08 am That last one is not a poster but the boxart from the Bandai Perfect Grade Eva-01 model kit. Specifically that's the version of the image that appears in the Die Sterne artbook because it has the elements rearranged. Here is the actual box:

[]:             Quote Post by Ari » Tue Jul 07, 2015 12:07 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: That last one is not a poster but the boxart from the Bandai Perfect Grade Eva-01 model kit. Specifically that's the version of the image that appears in the Die Sterne artbook because it has the elements rearranged. Here is the actual box:

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Jul 07, 2015 12:18 pm Ari wrote: Jeez, I can't trust any of these Eva poster sellers >.<

[]:             Quote Post by Ari » Tue Jul 07, 2015 7:39 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: You would be extremely hard pressed to find a legitimate source for official posters in the west. Everything gets scanned, shopped, and reprinted.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Tue Jul 07, 2015 9:50 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: You would be extremely hard pressed to find a legitimate source for official posters in the west.

There's a company called Asylum Anime that specializes in authentic Japanese posters and other imported goods and can hunt down vintage stuff for the right price. As regular dealers at Otakon, they've supplied the vast majority of my poster collection. Note that the similarly-named Anime Asylum is a different company. An old otaku that I know also once recommended Anime Jungle as another company that could hunt down import goods. I have a 1997 Japanese theatrical EoE poster that I've debated selling as well, btw.
